data.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion E4 — Assumption/premise verification), Cluster 1 plan.

AFFIRMATIONS (codebase assertions verified by Read/Grep — training knowledge not used):
- "src/rebar/_store/canonical.py is the single home for sorted-key compact JSON" + "6 existing event-serialization importers": VERIFIED. canonical.py:39-47 defines canonical_str/canonical_bytes with ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"). Grep confirms exactly 6 src importers (event_append.py:33, delete.py:34, compact.py:28, txn.py:41, inbound_translate.py:218, batch_dispatch.py:211) — matches the claim.
- "byte-parity contract test ... unaffected": VERIFIED. tests/unit/test_canonical.py:22 + tests/interfaces/store/test_canonical_event_bytes.py:22 import canonical_bytes positionally; adding keyword-only ascii_only/default params with byte-preserving defaults leaves them green.
- signing.py _canonical_payload "already uses the canonical kwargs → byte-identical": VERIFIED at signing.py:187-199 (sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False).
- schema.py canonical_json/content_hash + "preserve llm/workflow/__init__.py __all__": VERIFIED. schema.py:390-403 define both (ensure_ascii=False; content_hash = sha256 of canonical_json). __init__.py:56-57,85-86 export both and list them in __all__:69.
- conflict_resolver._hash_value "default=str (non-JSON-native provenance values)": VERIFIED at conflict_resolver.py:215-219 (json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",",":"))).
- mutation.serialize_manifest "ensure_ascii=True (preserve, do NOT normalize)": VERIFIED at mutation.py:106 (json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=True)).
- Cross-package import path (mutation.py/conflict_resolver.py live under rebar_reconciler but plan imports rebar._store.canonical): VERIFIED reachable — sibling modules already do exactly this (inbound_translate.py:218, batch_dispatch.py:211: `from rebar._store.canonical import canonical_str`), so the seam import works from this package.

No E4 findings. Every codebase assertion in the plan is grounded and confirmed against the named implementation files; no unverifiable/fail-closed gaps. Clean for this criterion.
